key_insight: D-gun deposited Al2O3-TiO2 coating significantly improves the tribological wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest best predicts wear from test conditions.
engineering_relevance: This work supports using thermal-sprayed ceramic coatings to extend the service life of titanium alloy components in aerospace, biomedical, and marine applications.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 coating on Ti-6Al-4V using detonation gun spray.
Evaluated wear with ASTM G99 pin-on-disc tests at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Built ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from sliding parameters.
evidence_signal: SEM observations showed reduced wear and a protective tribo-layer on coated samples, while random forest gave the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error among the tested models.
watch_item: The machine-learning results appear based on a limited experimental range, so broader validation is needed before using the model for new operating conditions.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
